name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: Evidence shows an access review was performed for each in-scope system during the audit period
The frequency of reviews is at least quarterly (or matches the documented cadence)
The date of each review is documented and falls within the expected review window
The review covers the complete population of users with access to the in-scope system at the time of review

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: The reviewer/approver is identified by name for each access review
The reviewer is the designated system or data owner (or an appropriate delegate) for the system in scope
The reviewer is independent of the users whose access is being reviewed (i.e. not self-reviewing their own access)
Evidence of formal approval (e.g. sign-off, ticket, signed report) is retained for each review

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: All access items flagged as inappropriate or excessive during the review are identified and tracked
Each flagged item has evidence of remediation (e.g. access removed or modified)
Remediation occurred within a defined timeframe following identification
Evidence confirms the flagged access no longer exists in the system post-remediation
